name: Tofu Burgers #2
recipeIngredient: 14 oz Tofu, firm; drained and rinsed
recipeInstructions:
1. Wrap tofu in a kitchen towel or several layers of paper towels. Place on a cutting board and weight with an iron skillet for 30 to 45 minutes.
2. Meanwhile, sauté onion and garlic in oil until the onion is limp and slightly brown on the edges.
3. Remove the towels and place the tofu in a large bowl. Mash with a fork and add the onion-garlic mixture.
4. Add chili sauce, Worcestershire sauce, soy sauce, cumin, bread crumbs (or hazelnuts), and mix well. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Refrigerate for 30 minutes.
5. With wet hands, shape tofu mixture into 8 to 10 patties and place on a well-oiled vegetable grill.
6. Handle patties gently; they are delicate. Grill for 3 to 4 minutes on each side until browned.
- Variation: Instead of grilling, sauté burgers in olive oil until lightly browned on both sides.
7. Note: This burger is especially tasty when served with a spicy sauce made of low-sodium soy sauce mixed with chili sauce or barbecue sauce and topped with sauteed mushrooms.
Per patty: 158 calories; 9g protein; 6g fat; 16g carbs; 0 cholesterol; 359mg sodium; 1g fiber
